questions.question: In Dart, every class implicitly defines an interface.
questions.explanation: Correct! You can 'implement' any class in Dart without needing a separate interface keyword.

questions.question: The 'late' keyword is used to declare a variable that will be initialized later.
questions.explanation: It tells the compiler that the non-nullable variable will have a value before it is used.

questions.question: Dart is a single-threaded language by nature (using Isolate for concurrency).
questions.explanation: Dart code runs in an isolate which has its own memory and a single thread running an event loop.

questions.question: You use the 'extends' keyword for mixins in Dart.
questions.explanation: Mixins are added to a class using the 'with' keyword, not 'extends'.

questions.question: A 'Future' represents a value that will be available at some point in the future.
questions.explanation: Futures are used for asynchronous operations like network calls or file reading.

questions.question: Variables starting with an underscore (e.g., _name) are private to their library.
questions.explanation: In Dart, leading underscore indicates privacy at the library/file level.
